observer_contract: Coordinate times are emitted by HaloBank time-core as UTC/TAI/TT/TDB/TCB; proper time remains an observer-derived quantity and is not promoted above diagnostic tier in this subsystem.
signal_contract: Timelike and null observable proofs share the same weak-field solar metric context; route-level aberration controls remain kinematic transport options, not a separate gravity model.

standards.citation: IAU 2000 Resolution B1.3, relativistic framework for BCRS and GCRS.
IAU 2006 Resolution B3, TDB as a linear function of TCB.
IERS Conventions (2010), reference systems and relativistic time transformations.
Einstein (1916), weak-field light deflection and GR field-equation era foundation.
Bertotti, Iess, Tortora (2003), Cassini PPN gamma constraint from Shapiro delay.

source_potentials.role: IAU barycentric scalar potential used in the weak-field metric definition.
IAU barycentric vector potential for first post-Newtonian terms.
IAU geocentric scalar potential used when the request is expressed in GCRS.
IAU geocentric vector potential for first post-Newtonian terms.
Dominant solar weak-field source term for Mercury perihelion precession, solar light deflection, and Shapiro delay diagnostics.
